    closed form `c┬Ęr^a/(1-r)` instead of staying residual)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (series / Phase-6-adjacent calculus)
  - cell: `sum(1/2^k,k,0,inf)ŌåÆ2`, `sum(1/3^k,k,0,inf)ŌåÆ3/2`, `sum((1/2)^k,k,1,inf)ŌåÆ1`,
    `sum(2*(1/3)^k,k,0,inf)ŌåÆ3`, `sum((-1/2)^k,k,0,inf)ŌåÆ2/3` (alternating),
    `sum(3^(-k),k,0,inf)ŌåÆ3/2`, `sum(5/4^k,k,2,inf)ŌåÆ5/12`. Divergent (`2^k`, `(3/2)^k`)ŌåÆ
    `infinity`; oscillating (`(-2)^k`) and non-geometric (`k┬Ę2^k`, `2^k+3^k`) stay residual.
  - primary_dimension: completeness (infinite series)
  - behavior_change_expected: convergent geometric flips from residual to closed form
    (updates the explicit "stays UNEVALUATED" contract); a new `ConvergentInfinite` sum kind
    narrated "Convergent Geometric Series". Calculus guardrail+pressure huella structurally
    NONE; workspace+clippy+fmt green.
- observed (frontier probe: `sum(1/2^k,k,0,inf)` residual while divergent geometric already
  classified to `infinity`):
  - `classify_infinite_sum` handled constant/polynomial/`r>1`-base divergence but explicitly
    left `|r|<1` UNEVALUATED. The closed form is standard; the only subtlety is the summand's
    normalised form ŌĆö `(1/2)^k`, `1/2^k`, `3^(-k)` all reach the matcher as `Pow`, `Div`, or
    `b^(s┬Ęk+t)` shapes.
  - `extract_geometric_term` is a STRUCTURAL matcher returning `(coeff, ratio)` from
    `b^(s┬Ęk+t)` (integer `s`,`t` keep `r=b^s`, `c=b^t` rational, covering reciprocals and
    negative bases), `Div(const, geometric)`, `Mul(const, geometric)`, and `Neg`. The
    structural match is the PROOF the term is geometric ŌĆö `k┬Ę2^k` / `2^k+3^k` don't match.
- retained learning:
  - prefer a STRUCTURAL geometric matcher over a numeric ratio test: matching the
    `c┬Ęb^(s┬Ęk+t)` shape (with `s`,`t` integers) is exact and sound ŌĆö it both extracts the
    ratio and CERTIFIES the term is purely geometric, so a non-geometric summand can't slip
    through (a 2-point numeric ratio test could be fooled). The convergence gate `|r|<1` is
    an exact `BigRational` comparison.
  - adding a new `SumEvaluationKind` variant requires arms in FOUR match sites across three
    crates (the kindŌåÆlabel in arithmetic.rs, the profiling-label match, and the step
    descriptors in cas_engine summation.rs + cas_solver derive_command.rs); the compiler's
    non-exhaustive-match errors enumerate them ŌĆö let it drive the wiring.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): symbolic lower bound (`sum((1/2)^k,k,m,inf)=2^(1-m)`), finite
    geometric with fractional ratio (`sum(1/2^k,k,0,n)` still residual), and non-geometric
    convergent series (p-series `1/k┬▓=ŽĆ┬▓/6`, telescoping-to-constant).

## 2026-06-21 - Retained capability: finite geometric sum with general rational ratio
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`try_build_geometric_rational_sum`, wired
    into `try_plan_finite_sum_evaluation` after the integer-base `try_build_geometric_power_sum`)
- status:
  - `retained` (capability: `sum(c┬Ęr^k, k, a, n)` with a fractional/negative rational ratio
    and a symbolic OR numeric upper bound now closes; completes the previous cycle's
    geometric series work to the finite case)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (series / Phase-6-adjacent)
  - cell: `sum(1/2^k,k,0,n) = 2 - 2┬Ę(1/2)^(n+1)`, `sum((2/3)^k,k,1,n) = 2 - 3┬Ę(2/3)^(n+1)`,
    `sum(1/3^k,k,0,n) = 3/2┬Ę(1-(1/3)^(n+1))`, `sum((-1/2)^k,k,0,n) = 2/3┬Ę(1-(-1/2)^(n+1))`.
    Integer-base finite (`sum(2^k,k,0,n) = 2^(n+1)-1`) is UNCHANGED (existing builder runs
    first). Closed form verified exact against brute force at large numeric bounds
    (0 mismatches over 25 ratio├Śbound cases).
  - behavior_change_expected: fractional/negative-ratio finite geometric flips from residual
    to closed form (reuses the `GeometricPower` kind, no new narration). Calculus
    guardrail+pressure huella structurally NONE; workspace+clippy+fmt green.
- observed:
  - the existing `try_build_geometric_power_sum` only matched `Pow(integerŌēź2, k)`. Placing a
    general-ratio builder AFTER it (reusing the shared `extract_geometric_term`) catches the
    declined fractional/negative ratios while leaving the integer-base output byte-identical
    ŌĆö the standard formula `c┬Ę(r^a - r^(n+1))/(1-r)` reproduces `2^(n+1)-1` for `r=2`, so
    even if the ordering changed the value would agree.
- retained learning:
  - to extend a family without huella churn, add the new branch AFTER the incumbent so the
    already-covered shapes keep their exact existing output; the new branch only ever runs
    on inputs the incumbent declined.
  - VERIFY a symbolic closed form by substituting the bound and folding to an exact rational,
    NOT by re-parsing the display: the formatter renders `(2/3)^m` as the ambiguous `2/3^m`
    (a pre-existing display quirk), so a sweep that re-parses the display gets the wrong
    value while the internal `Pow(2/3, ┬Ę)` is correct. `as_rational_const` (both the
    `cas_ast::views` and `numeric_eval` variants) does NOT fold `Pow`, so a power-aware fold
    helper is needed for the test.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): symbolic LOWER bound (`sum((1/2)^k,k,m,inf)` / `ŌĆ”,m,n)`), and the
    display ambiguity `(p/q)^n ŌåÆ p/q^n` is a formatter gap worth a separate cosmetic cycle.

## 2026-06-21 - Soundness fix: poly gcd command returned 1 for expanded polynomials
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/poly_gcd_dispatch.rs` (`try_univariate_euclidean_gcd`; the
    `GcdMode::Structural` arm of `compute_poly_gcd_unified_with` now falls back to it)
- status:
  - `retained` (soundness/correctness: `gcd(x^3-1, x^2-1)` returned `1` ŌĆö falsely claiming
    coprime ŌĆö and now returns the correct `x-1`)
- capture:
  - investment_class: soundness (wrong-answer fix)
  - cell: `gcd(x^3-1, x^2-1) = x-1`, `gcd(x^2-1, x-1) = x-1`, `gcd(x^2-4, x^2-x-2) = x-2`,
    `gcd(x^3-x^2-2x, x^3+x^2+x+1) = x+1` (clean monic, not a `5/4┬Ęx+5/4` scale). Coprime
    pairs (`gcd(x, x+1)`, `gcd(x^2+1, x-1)`) correctly stay `1`; numeric (`gcd(6,4)=2`) and
    factored-form structural fast path (`gcd((x-1)(x+1), (x-1)(x+2)) = x-1`) unchanged.
  - behavior_change_expected: `gcd`/`poly_gcd` of EXPANDED univariate polynomials flips from
    a wrong `1` to the correct gcd. Calculus guardrail+pressure huella structurally NONE;
    fraction-cancellation domain conditions unchanged (strict `(x^2-1)/(x-1)` still keeps
    `x-1 ŌēĀ 0` and declines). Workspace + clippy + fmt green.
- observed:
  - `poly_gcd_structural` only collects shared `Mul` factors, so for an EXPANDED pair (which
    has no product structure) it returns `1` ŌĆö claiming coprime when the true gcd is `x-1`.
    BOTH `gcd(...)` (number-theory dispatch) and `poly_gcd(...)` route through this default
    Structural mode, so the wrong answer was user-visible on both. The `exact` mode was
    already correct (`poly_gcd(..., exact) = x-1`), and `Polynomial::gcd` (Euclidean) is
    correct too ŌĆö the bug was purely the default mode not using them.
  - fix: when structural returns `1`, fall back to the primitive Euclidean `Polynomial::gcd`
    for univariate (clean, exact) and `gcd_exact` for multivariate. Made the univariate
    result MONIC by dividing by the leading coefficient, because `Polynomial::content()`
    returns `1` for non-integer coefficients so the Euclidean result can carry an arbitrary
    rational scale (`5/4┬Ę(x+1)`); monic normalisation gives the canonical `x+1`.
- retained learning:
  - a "structural"/syntactic shortcut that returns a UNIT (`1` for gcd, `0` for a difference)
    as its not-found fallback is UNSOUND when a real answer exists ŌĆö it must fall back to the
    complete algorithm (here Euclidean/exact), not silently report coprime. Structural-only
    matchers belong as a fast path with a correct fallback, never as the whole answer.
  - `Polynomial::content()` is a known stub for non-integer coefficients (returns `1`), so
    `Polynomial::gcd`'s "primitive" normalisation can leave a rational scale; normalise gcd
    output to MONIC (divide by leading coeff) for a canonical form.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): multivariate `gcd` clean-form via the exact path, and `gcd`/`pgcd`
    of more-than-two arguments.

## 2026-06-21 - Higher-order and mixed-partial diff syntax: diff(f, x, n) / diff(f, x, y)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/symbolic_calculus_call_support.rs` (`try_desugar_higher_order_diff`)
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/rules/calculus/diff_rule.rs` (`HigherOrderDiffRule`) + registration in
    `rules/calculus/mod.rs`
  - `crates/cas_session_core/src/eval.rs` (arity gate `"diff" => arity >= 2`)
- status:
  - `retained` (new Phase-1 capability: the cheapest, most visible P1 win in
    `CALCULUS_ENGINE_DEVELOPMENT_PHASES.md`)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (new public syntax)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 elementary differentiation)
  - cell: `diff(x^4, x, 2) = 12┬Ęx^2`, `diff(sin(x), x, 4) = sin(x)`, `diff(exp(x), x, 3) = e^x`,
    `diff(x^3*y^2, x, y) = 6┬Ęy┬Ęx^2` (mixed partial Ōłé┬▓/ŌłéyŌłéx), `diff(x^3*y^2, x, x) = 6┬Ęx┬Ęy^2`,
    `diff(x^3*y^2, x, 2, y, 2) = 12┬Ęx` (SymPy mixed-count syntax). `diff(f, x, 1)` folds to the
    ordinary first derivative; `diff(f, x)` and the 1-arg error are unchanged.
  - rejects (honest, left unevaluated ŌĆö never a wrong answer): `diff(f, 2, x)` (a count may not
    lead the variable list), `diff(f, x, 0)` / `diff(f, x, -1)` (order < 1), `diff(f, x, 1/2)`
    (fractional/non-integer order ŌĆö fractional calculus is out of scope).
  - behavior_change_expected: `diff` with 3+ args flips from the targeted
    "diff requiere variable expl├Łcita" error to the evaluated higher-order / mixed-partial
    derivative. Guardrail+pressure huella structurally NONE except
    `calculus_integrate_backend_mode_boundary.filtered_out 2220 -> 2225` (+5 = the five new
    cas_engine unit tests sharing that test binary; a harness bookkeeping count, not an engine
    metric) and timing-noise in `derive_didactic_audit` hotspot lists. Workspace 12229 passed /
    0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the whole differentiation pipeline (presentation routes + generic fallback) keys off a
    two-argument `NamedVarCall` from `try_extract_diff_call`, which only matches `args.len()==2`.
    Rather than thread an order through every route, desugar `diff(f, x, n)` / `diff(f, x, y)`
    into nested two-argument `diff(diff(...))` so each layer flows through the EXISTING (already
    trusted) machinery. `diff(f, x, 3) == diff(diff(diff(f, x), x), x)` verified byte-identical
    against manual nesting for `x^6`, `cos`, `e^(2x)`, `1/x`, and a bivariate target.
  - SymPy count semantics implemented uniformly: walk the arg tail; a Variable contributes one
    step and becomes the "current" variable; a positive-integer literal repeats the current
    variable `n-1` more times (it was already applied once). An integer leading the tail has no
    variable to repeat ŌåÆ reject.
- retained learning:
  - to add a higher-arity / variadic form of an existing rule family, DESUGAR to the form the
    family already trusts (nested two-argument calls here) instead of teaching every route the
    new arity. The new behavior is then correct by construction and adds ~zero risk to the
    incumbent paths ŌĆö the existing 2-arg routes are reached unchanged.
  - a "new capability" cycle can still be huella-clean: routing through the trusted lower-arity
    path leaves every existing fixture byte-identical; the only structural delta is the
    test-binary `filtered_out` bookkeeping from the new unit tests, which is expected and named.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): `diff(f, x, 0) = f` and fractional/symbolic order are deliberately left
    as honest residuals; higher-order *step narration* (showing each successive derivative) is a
    separate educational cycle ŌĆö today only the final form is shown.

## 2026-06-21 - Summation linearity: sum of any polynomial summand by Faulhaber per monomial
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`try_build_polynomial_sum`, helpers
    `power_sum_one_to` / `power_sum_start_to_end`, `SumEvaluationKind::PolynomialLinearity`,
    dispatched LAST in `try_plan_finite_sum_evaluation`)
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/rules/calculus/summation.rs` + `rules/arithmetic.rs` and
    `crates/cas_solver/src/derive_command.rs` (the three exhaustive `SumEvaluationKind` matches)
- status:
  - `retained` (new Phase-1 capability: the "linealidad de sumatorios" P1 win;
    series was ~28%, the lowest in-scope chapter)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (new closed forms)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `sum(2k, k, 1, n) = n(n+1)`, `sum(k^2+k, k, 1, n)`, `sum(3k^2, k, 1, n)`,
    `sum(k+1, k, 1, n)`, `sum(k(k+1), k, 1, n)` (product expanded), `sum(3k^2-k+1, k, 1, n)`,
    and symbolic start `sum(2k, k, m, n) = m(1-m) + n(n+1)`. Degree > 3 (`sum(k^4)`) and
    non-polynomial (`sum(1/k)`, `sum(2^k)`) stay residual / owned by other builders.
  - behavior_change_expected: scaled / multi-term polynomial sums with a SYMBOLIC upper bound
    flip from unevaluated to a closed form. Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE
    (this builder only fires on shapes the dedicated builders declined; bare `k`/`k^2`/`k^3`,
    constants, geometric, and finite-numeric ranges keep their exact existing output).
    Workspace 12232 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the closed-form dispatch matched only BARE shapes (`k`, `k^2`, `k^3`, constant, geometric);
    any coefficient or extra term fell through to unevaluated. Adding a polynomial fallback that
    reads the summand as a `Polynomial` in the index and sums each monomial `a_p┬Ęk^p` by its
    Faulhaber closed form (`╬Żk = n(n+1)/2`, `╬Żk^2`, `╬Żk^3`) covers the whole linear-combination
    family at once, reusing the EXACT expressions the single-power builders emit.
  - placed LAST in the dispatch (after the single-power, constant, geometric, and
    finite-numeric builders) so every already-owned shape keeps byte-identical output; the new
    branch only ever runs on inputs the incumbents declined ŌĆö hence huella-NONE.
  - `Polynomial::from_expr` expands products, so `k(k+1)` is summed as `k^2+k` for free;
    symbolic lower bounds work via `╬Ż_{start}^{end} = ╬Ż_1^{end} ŌłÆ ╬Ż_1^{start-1}` (the `k=0`
    term vanishes for `p Ōēź 1`, matching the existing builders' startŌłł{0,1} fast path).
- retained learning:
  - a closed-form family keyed on BARE shapes generalises cheaply and huella-safely by adding a
    Polynomial-based linearity fallback AFTER the specific builders: linearity over a basis whose
    elements you already close (here `╬Żk^p`) covers every linear combination with one matcher.
  - verify a symbolic summation closed form by an INDEPENDENT brute force ŌĆö substitute the bound
    and fold to an exact rational, compare to `╬Ż_{k=start}^{n} poly(k)` summed term by term in
    plain Rust over a coefficient/bound sweep. This catches bound off-by-ones and coefficient
    mismatches that a single hand-checked example would miss.
  - adding a `SumEvaluationKind` variant touches THREE exhaustive matches (engine arithmetic
    label + didactic render, solver derive render); the compiler's E0004 lists each ŌĆö fix them
    all rather than adding a wildcard that would silently mislabel future variants.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): degree Ōēź 4 needs general Faulhaber (Bernoulli numbers); `sum(k┬Ę2^k)`
    (arithmetic-geometric) and `sum(1/(k(k+1)))`-style are separate families.

## 2026-06-21 - Expose taylor()/series() command over the internal Maclaurin engine
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/limits_support.rs` (`taylor_series_at_zero_expr` public wrapper over
    the existing private `taylor_at_zero`)
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/rules/calculus/taylor.rs` (NEW: `TaylorRule` + `try_extract_taylor_call`)
    + registration in `rules/calculus/mod.rs`
  - `crates/cas_session_core/src/eval.rs` (arity gate) + `env.rs` (RESERVED_NAMES)
- status:
  - `retained` (new Phase-1 capability: the `taylor()`/`series()` half of the
    "taylor/series + summation linearity" P1 win; the summation half landed earlier today)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (expose existing engine as a public command)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `taylor(exp(x), x, 0, 4) = 1 + x + x^2/2 + x^3/6 + x^4/24`,
    `taylor(sin(x), x, 0, 5)` (= x - x^3/6 + x^5/120), `taylor(cos(x), x, 4)`,
    `taylor(ln(1+x), x, 0, 4)`, `taylor(tan(x), x, 0, 5)` (= x + x^3/3 + 2x^5/15);
    `series(...)` is an alias and the 3-arg form `taylor(f, x, n)` defaults the point to 0.
  - rejects (honest residual, left unevaluated): non-zero expansion point (`taylor(f, x, 1, n)`),
    negative/non-integer order, and non-analytic-at-0 summands (`1/x`, `1/(1-x)` rational
    functions ŌĆö the Maclaurin engine covers exp/sin/cos/sinh/cosh/tan/atan/asin/ln + polys,
    products, integer powers, compositions, but not Div / negative powers).
  - behavior_change_expected: `taylor(...)`/`series(...)` flip from "funci├│n no definida" to the
    truncated Taylor polynomial. Guardrail huella structurally NONE except
    `calculus_integrate_backend_mode_boundary.filtered_out 2225 -> 2228` (+3 = the three new
    cas_engine taylor unit tests in that binary); pressure NONE. Limits that share
    `taylor_at_zero` ((sin x - x)/x^3 -> -1/6, (1-cos x)/x^2 -> 1/2) unchanged. Workspace 12235
    passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the analytic Maclaurin engine already existed as the PRIVATE `taylor_at_zero` (built for the
    L'Hopital/Taylor limit quotient rule); exposing it was a thin public wrapper + a command
    rule, not new mathematics. The order argument means "max degree inclusive" (the engine
    truncates with `take(order + 1)`).
  - `taylor_at_zero` expands only at 0 and only over the analytic family; rational functions and
    non-zero points are genuinely unsupported, so the command declines them rather than
    fabricating ŌĆö consistent with the soundness discipline.
- retained learning:
  - before building a "new" capability, grep for an INTERNAL engine that already does the math
    for a neighbouring feature (here the limit evaluator's series engine): exposing it as a
    command is a thin wrapper + arity gate + reserved name, and inherits the trust the internal
    path already earned (the limit tests exercise it).
  - a command that wraps an internal partial engine must mirror its DOMAIN exactly in the
    extractor (point = 0 only, non-negative integer order) so unsupported inputs stay honest
    residuals instead of silently wrong or panicking.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): rational/geometric Taylor (`1/(1-x)`, `1/(1+x^2)`) via series division
    or the geometric expansion; non-zero expansion point `taylor(f, x, a, n)` via the shift
    `f(x) -> f(a + h)`; and a prettier per-term display (today `Polynomial::to_expr` factors a
    common denominator: `1/24┬Ę(x^4 + ... + 24)`).

## 2026-06-21 - Rational/geometric Taylor: series of 1/(1-x), 1/(1+x^2) for the taylor() command
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/limits_support.rs` (`taylor_at_zero_with_rational`, `reciprocal_series`;
    the public `taylor_series_at_zero_expr` wrapper now routes through the rational extension)
- status:
  - `retained` (extends today's `taylor()`/`series()` command to rational summands)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (series of rational functions)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `taylor(1/(1-x), x, 0, 4) = 1 + x + x^2 + x^3 + x^4`,
    `taylor(1/(1+x), x, 0, 4) = 1 - x + x^2 - x^3 + x^4`,
    `taylor(1/(1+x^2), x, 0, 6) = 1 - x^2 + x^4 - x^6`, `taylor(1/(2-x), x, 0, 3)`,
    `taylor(1/(1-x)^2, x, 0, 3) = 1 + 2x + 3x^2 + 4x^3`, `taylor(exp(x)/(1-x), x, 0, 3)`
    (analytic numerator ├Ś rational denominator). `1/x`, `1/x^2` (pole at 0) stay residual;
    `1/(x-x)` folds to `undefined`.
  - behavior_change_expected: `taylor()`/`series()` of a quotient or negative integer power flips
    from unevaluated to its Maclaurin polynomial. Guardrail huella structurally NONE except
    `calculus_integrate_backend_mode_boundary.filtered_out 2228 -> 2229` (+1 new cas_engine test);
    pressure NONE. **Limits are completely unaffected**: the rational extension is a SEPARATE
    function the command wrapper calls; the limit evaluator keeps calling the original
    `taylor_at_zero`. Workspace 12238 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - `taylor_at_zero` (the limit engine's series) only handled analytic forms; a quotient returned
    None. Added `taylor_at_zero_with_rational = taylor_at_zero + Div + negative-integer-Pow`,
    using a power-series reciprocal `1/den` (recurrence `r_0 = 1/d_0`,
    `r_k = -(1/d_0)╬Ż d_i r_{k-i}`) which requires `den(0) ŌēĀ 0` and so DECLINES poles at 0.
  - keeping the rational branch in a separate function (not folded into `taylor_at_zero`) is what
    makes it huella-safe: the limit quotient rule, which calls `taylor_at_zero` on numerator and
    denominator independently, sees no change.
- retained learning:
  - when extending an internal engine that a DIFFERENT feature also depends on, branch the
    extension into a new function the new caller routes through, leaving the shared callers on the
    original. Isolation by call graph (not by flag) is the cleanest way to add capability with
    zero regression risk to the incumbent consumer ŌĆö confirmed here by limits staying byte-identical.
  - the power-series reciprocal recurrence is the one primitive that unlocks ALL rational Taylor
    (quotients and negative powers reduce to it); gating it on `den(0) ŌēĀ 0` is exactly the
    analyticity condition, so poles decline for free.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): non-zero expansion point `taylor(f, x, a, n)` (shift `f(a+h)`); series of
    `arctan`/`asin` via their derivative's geometric series if ever wanted as closed sums; and the
    transcendental u-substitution integration win (`cos(x)┬Ęe^(sin x)`) remains open ŌĆö it needs a
    sound exact verifier in the 29k-line block-12 backend, a properly-scoped larger cycle, not a
    bounded one.

## 2026-06-21 - Integrate p(x)┬Ęa^x by parts for a general constant base
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/symbolic_integration_support.rs`
    (`polynomial_times_constant_base_power_antiderivative`, registered in the main symbolic
    integration dispatch right after the e^(linear) by-parts kernel;
    `integrate_symbolic_is_polynomial_times_constant_base_power_target` predicate)
- status:
  - `retained` (new Phase-1 capability: the `x┬Ęa^x` by-parts P2 win; the `a^x/ln(a)` table
    already integrated, the polynomial cofactor case was residual for any base ŌēĀ e)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (new integration family)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 elementary integration)
  - cell: `integrate(x┬Ę2^x) = x┬Ę2^x/ln(2) ŌłÆ 2^x/ln(2)^2`, `integrate(x┬Ę3^x)`, `integrate(x^2┬Ę2^x)`,
    `integrate((x+1)┬Ę2^x)`, `integrate(x┬Ę(3/2)^x)`, and the definite
    `integrate(x┬Ę2^x, 0, 1) = (2ln2 ŌłÆ 1)/ln(2)^2`. Round-trip `diff(integrate(p┬Ęa^x)) = p┬Ęa^x`
    holds for every probed case.
  - behavior_change_expected: `polynomial ┬Ę a^x` (constant rational base a > 0, a ŌēĀ 1, exponent
    exactly the variable, cofactor degree 1..8) flips from residual to its by-parts antiderivative.
    Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE (no existing fixture covered this shape; the new
    unit test lives in cas_math, not the cas_engine binary). Workspace 12239 passed / 0 failed;
    clippy/fmt green.
  - adversarial: round-trip diff verified for linear/quadratic/cubic cofactors, fractional base,
    negative cofactor, scaled cofactor. Honest residuals (no mis-fire): exponent ŌēĀ x
    (`2^(2x)`, `2^(x+1)`), zero base (`0^x`), negative base (`(-2)^x`, no real antiderivative),
    base 1 (folds to a polynomial), and two power factors (`2^x┬Ę3^x` folds to `6^x` ŌåÆ table).
- observed:
  - `a^x = e^(x┬Ęln a)`, so the by-parts antiderivative is `a^x┬Ę╬Ż_{k} (-1)^k p^(k)(x)/(ln a)^(k+1)` ŌĆö
    the SAME series shape as the e^(cx) kernel, but the "slope" is the SYMBOLIC `ln a`, not a
    rational `c`. The existing kernel (`polynomial_exp_by_parts_inner`) builds a `Polynomial`
    with a `BigRational` slope and cannot represent `1/(ln a)^k`, so a parallel matcher that
    builds the sum as a symbolic expression (powers of `ln(a)` in the denominators) was required.
  - the antiderivative is EXACT by construction (the by-parts identity), so no separate verifier
    is needed; the round-trip diff check is confirmation, not the soundness gate.
- retained learning:
  - a rational-slope by-parts kernel does NOT generalise to a symbolic slope by parameter swap:
    the inner accumulator is a `Polynomial` (rational coefficients), but `1/(ln a)^k` is not a
    rational coefficient. The symbolic-slope sibling must build the result as an EXPRESSION tree,
    not a `Polynomial` ŌĆö a clean parallel matcher beats contorting the polynomial kernel.
  - exact guards make a structural integration matcher sound without a verifier: positive rational
    base ŌēĀ 0,1 (excludes `(-2)^x`/`0^x`/`1^x`), exponent EXACTLY the variable (excludes affine
    exponents that would need a slope factor), polynomial cofactor of degree Ōēź 1 (degree 0 is the
    table's job). Each guard maps to a real soundness or ownership boundary.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): affine exponent `a^(cx+d)` (needs the e^(cx) slope handling carried into
    the a^x outer factor); and integrate-command-matrix rows for this family are deferred ŌĆö the
    matrix's ~15 interdependent counter assertions make row-addition its own bounded task, and the
    capability is already locked by the round-trip-verified unit test.

## 2026-06-21 - General Faulhaber: polynomial sums of any degree (lifts the cycle-2 degree-3 cap)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`faulhaber_power_sum_coeffs`, `binomial`,
    `horner_power_sum_expr`; `power_sum_one_to` extended to `p Ōēź 4`; the degree cap in
    `try_build_polynomial_sum` lifted from 3 to `MAX_POLYNOMIAL_SUM_DEGREE = 12`)
- status:
  - `retained` (closes the explicit degree-Ōēż3 residual left by the 2026-06-21 summation-linearity
    cycle; `sum(k^4)`, `sum(k^5+k^2)`, ŌĆ” now have closed forms with a symbolic bound)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (higher-degree closed forms)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `sum(k^4, k, 1, n) = n^5/5 + n^4/2 + n^3/3 ŌłÆ n/30`, `sum(k^5, k, 1, n)`,
    `sum(k^4+k^2, k, 1, n)`, `sum(k^6)`, ŌĆ” up to degree 12; finite numeric checks
    (k^4 1..3 = 98, k^5 1..4 = 1300, k^7 1..2 = 129) all match.
  - behavior_change_expected: polynomial summands of degree 4..12 with a symbolic bound flip from
    residual to their Faulhaber closed form. Degree > 12 stays residual (a deliberate bound on the
    closed-form size, not a math limit). Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE ŌĆö p Ōēż 3
    keeps its byte-identical factored output (the dedicated `power_sum_one_to` arms are untouched);
    only p Ōēź 4 uses the new recurrence path. Contract test updated: `sum(k^4)` now plans as
    `PolynomialLinearity` instead of residual. Workspace 12240 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the cycle-2 linearity path summed each monomial via `power_sum_one_to`, which only knew the
    hard-coded `╬Żk`, `╬Żk^2`, `╬Żk^3`. The Faulhaber recurrence
    `(p+1)S_p = (n+1)^(p+1) ŌłÆ 1 ŌłÆ ╬Ż_{j<p} C(p+1,j)S_j` (with `S_0 = n`) computes `S_p(n)` as an
    exact rational-coefficient polynomial from the lower sums; Horner-building it at the bound
    expression closes any degree.
  - keeping `p Ōēż 3` on the original factored expressions (not the recurrence) is what keeps the
    huella byte-identical: `sum(2k)`/`sum(k^2+k)`/`sum(k^3)` render exactly as before.
- retained learning:
  - a closed-form family capped at small degree generalises to ALL degree by the standard
    recurrence over its own lower members (Faulhaber here); add the general path ONLY for the
    degrees the hard-coded forms don't cover, so the already-shipped low-degree output stays
    byte-identical and the huella never moves.
  - verify a degree-parametric closed form by substituting the bound and folding to an exact
    rational vs an independent brute force, swept over BOTH degree (4..8) and bounds ŌĆö a single
    degree or a single bound would miss recurrence and start-offset errors.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): degree > 12 (raise the cap if ever needed ŌĆö the recurrence is O(p┬▓)); and
    arithmetic-geometric `╬Ż k┬Ęr^k` / `╬Ż k^2┬Ęr^k`, a separate family from pure polynomial sums.

## 2026-06-21 - G2 gatekeeper (l├Łmites educativos) sub-ciclo 1: nombrar los l├Łmites notables
- area:
  - `crates/cas_didactic/src/didactic/focused_rule_substeps.rs` (`generate_limit_substeps`,
    `notable_limit_name` + matchers); dispatched in
    `enrichment_pipeline/rule_dispatch.rs`; whitelisted past the same-snapshot pruner in
    `enrichment_pipeline/step_loop.rs`
- status:
  - `retained` (primer sub-ciclo retenible del gatekeeper L "narrativa educativa de l├Łmites";
    los l├Łmites pasaban de un paso-c├Īscara ├║nico a nombrar el m├®todo)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (educational narration ŌĆö la mitad EDUCATIVA del north star)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, gatekeeper G2)
  - cell: `limit(sin(x)/x, x, 0)` ahora narra el substep "Aplicar el l├Łmite notable:
    l├Łm(uŌåÆ0) sin(u)/u = 1"; igual para `tan(u)/u ŌåÆ 1`, `(e^uŌłÆ1)/u ŌåÆ 1`, `ln(1+u)/u ŌåÆ 1`,
    `(1ŌłÆcos u)/u┬▓ ŌåÆ 1/2`. El valor del l├Łmite y el rule_name ("Evaluar l├Łmite finito") no cambian.
  - behavior_change_expected: los pasos de l├Łmite finito GANAN un substep que nombra el l├Łmite
    notable cuando aplica. Huella guardrail+pressure estructuralmente NONE (solo se a├▒aden
    substeps; valores, reglas y contadores id├®nticos). Workspace 12242 passed / 0 failed;
    clippy/fmt verdes.
  - SOUNDNESS: el substep se emite SOLO cuando el RESULTADO es el valor notable y la forma
    estructural casa exactamente ŌĆö `limit(sin(x)/x, x, 5) = sin(5)/5` (valor ŌēĀ 1) y
    `sin(2x)/x ŌåÆ 2` (forma escalada) NO se narran como el notable. Sin fabricaci├│n.
- observed:
  - la narraci├│n de diff vive en el pipeline de enriquecimiento de `cas_didactic`, que
    RECONSTRUYE el m├®todo desde el before/after del paso (el motor de diff no graba el m├®todo).
    Los l├Łmites se narran igual: un generador que casa el patr├│n de `before` y exige que `after`
    sea el valor notable ŌĆö sin tocar el motor de l├Łmites (`limits_support`), as├Ł que cero riesgo
    de soundness sobre los valores.
  - los substeps wire vienen SOLO de `enriched.sub_steps` (el `Step` se ignora en
    `collect_step_payload_substeps`), as├Ł que la narraci├│n DEBE a├▒adirse en el pipeline de
    enriquecimiento, no en la creaci├│n del paso.
  - un substep "same snapshot" (mismo before/after que el paso) lo poda `prune_redundant_substeps`
    salvo whitelist; para los notables el naming ES el contenido did├Īctico, as├Ł que se whitelist-ea
    por `rule_name.starts_with("Evaluar l├Łmite")`.
- retained learning:
  - para narrar un comando que hoy es paso-c├Īscara, MIRROR el patr├│n de diff: un generador en el
    pipeline de enriquecimiento de `cas_didactic` que reconstruye el "c├│mo" desde el before/after,
    sin que el motor de c├│mputo grabe nada. Mantiene el motor (y su soundness) intacto.
  - una narraci├│n por reconocimiento de patr├│n es SOUND si exige que el RESULTADO coincida con el
    valor del patr├│n: el resultado act├║a de or├Īculo (sin(x)/xŌåÆ1 solo es el notable cuando da 1).
  - residual (pelda├▒o G2): siguientes sub-ciclos ŌĆö sustituci├│n directa por continuidad
    (`limit(x┬▓+1,x,2)=5` ŌåÆ "sustituir x=2"), factor-y-cancela (`(x┬▓ŌłÆ1)/(xŌłÆ1)`), y L'H├┤pital/Taylor
    narrados. Estos necesitan el PUNTO del l├Łmite (hoy ausente del paso: el `before` es la expr
    desnuda); el siguiente sub-ciclo lo cablear├Ī (envolver el before en `limit(expr,var,punto)` o
    pasar el punto al enriquecimiento).

## 2026-06-21 - G2 gatekeeper sub-ciclo 2: m├Īs l├Łmites notables + s├Īndwich + (a^uŌłÆ1)/u y (1+u)^(1/u)

- status:

    formas est├Īndar reconocidas)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (educational narration)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, gatekeeper G2)

    `(2^xŌłÆ1)/x ŌåÆ ln(2)`, `(3^xŌłÆ1)/x ŌåÆ ln(3)` (exponencial general); `(1+x)^(1/x) ŌåÆ e` (Euler);
    `x┬Ęsin(1/x)`, `x┬▓┬Ęcos(1/x) ŌåÆ 0` (s├Īndwich). Todos narran su m├®todo/teorema.
  - behavior_change_expected: los pasos de l├Łmite ganan substep para 9 formas est├Īndar m├Īs.
    Huella guardrail+pressure NONE (solo substeps; valores y reglas id├®nticos). Workspace 12242

    es `ln(2)` (no `ln(3)`); el s├Īndwich exige que el oscilador sin/cos tenga la variable en un
    DENOMINADOR (reciprocal), as├Ł que `x┬Ęsin(x) ŌåÆ 0` (continuidad, no s├Īndwich) NO se narra.
- observed:
  - los equivalentes de primer orden (sin/tan/arcsin/arctan/sinh/tanh ~ u) comparten el mismo
    patr├│n `f(u)/u ŌåÆ 1`: una tabla `first_order_equivalent_name(builtin)` los unifica.
  - distinguir el s├Īndwich genuino de la continuidad necesita inspeccionar el ARGUMENTO del
    oscilador: `x┬Ęsin(1/x)` (la variable en denominador ŌåÆ no converge ŌåÆ s├Īndwich) vs `x┬Ęsin(x)`
    (argumento continuo ŌåÆ simple producto de l├Łmites). `limit_var_in_denominator` lo decide.
  - cambiar `notable_limit_name` a devolver `Option<String>` (descripci├│n completa, con su propio
    prefijo) deja que el s├Īndwich y los notables convivan; la whitelist de poda se simplifica a
    "conservar cualquier substep de un paso de l├Łmite" (los controlo todos).
- retained learning:
  - una familia de narraciones por reconocimiento de patr├│n crece barata si el RESULTADO es el
    or├Īculo de soundness y los discriminantes estructurales (reciprocal-en-denominador) separan
    el caso genuino del hom├│nimo trivial (s├Īndwich vs continuidad).
  - residual (pelda├▒o G2): siguen pendientes los m├®todos que necesitan el PUNTO del l├Łmite
    (sustituci├│n directa por continuidad, factor-y-cancela, L'H├┤pital/Taylor narrados) ŌĆö el punto
    sigue ausente del paso; cablearlo es el siguiente sub-ciclo arquitect├│nico del gatekeeper.

## 2026-06-21 - G2 gatekeeper sub-ciclo 3: continuidad (sustituci├│n directa) y factor-y-cancela
- area:
  - `crates/cas_didactic/src/didactic/focused_rule_substeps.rs` (`notable_limit_name` +2 brazos:
    continuidad polin├│mica y factor-y-cancela; helpers `limit_single_var_name`,
    `limit_share_polynomial_factor`, `limit_is_polynomial`)
- status:
  - `retained` (tercer sub-ciclo del gatekeeper G2; cubre los DOS m├®todos m├Īs comunes de l├Łmites
    de curso intro: continuidad/sustituci├│n directa y 0/0 removible por factorizaci├│n)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (educational narration)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, gatekeeper G2)
  - cell: `limit(x┬▓+1, x, 2) = 5` y `limit(3xŌłÆ1, x, 5) = 14` narran "Sustituci├│n directa:
    el l├Łmite de un polinomio es su valor en el punto (continuidad)"; `(x┬▓ŌłÆ1)/(xŌłÆ1) ŌåÆ 2`,
    `(x┬▓ŌłÆ4)/(xŌłÆ2) ŌåÆ 4`, `(x┬│ŌłÆ1)/(xŌłÆ1) ŌåÆ 3` narran "Factorizar numerador y denominador y cancelar
    el factor com├║n antes de evaluar".
  - behavior_change_expected: los pasos de l├Łmite ganan substep para los dos m├®todos b├Īsicos.
    Huella guardrail+pressure NONE. Workspace 12244 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt verdes.
  - SOUNDNESS: continuidad solo para `before` polin├│mico (continuo en todo punto ŌåÆ el l├Łmite ES el
    valor, siempre cierto); factor-y-cancela solo cuando `gcd(num,den)` tiene grado Ōēź 1 (existe
    factor com├║n, detectable del `before` sin el punto). El mensaje de factor-y-cancela es NEUTRO
    (no afirma "0/0", que requerir├Ła el punto): la cancelaci├│n `(x┬▓ŌłÆ1)/(xŌłÆ1)=x+1` es algebraicamente
    v├Īlida en CUALQUIER punto ŌĆö verificado en x=5 (=6). Coprimos (`(x┬▓+1)/(xŌłÆ1)`, polo genuino) son
    "Conservar l├Łmite residual" (otra regla) ŌåÆ no narrados; constantes sin variable ŌåÆ no narrados.
- observed:
  - sin el punto del l├Łmite no se puede afirmar "0/0" (la misma `(x┬▓ŌłÆ1)/(xŌłÆ1)` en x=5 vale 6, no es
    indeterminado); pero el ACTO de factorizar-y-cancelar es v├Īlido en cualquier punto, as├Ł que un
    mensaje neutro es sound. La existencia del factor com├║n se decide con `Polynomial::gcd` sobre el
    `before` ŌĆö el resultado del motor confirma que el l├Łmite es finito.
  - continuidad para polinomios es universalmente sound (un polinomio es continuo en ŌäØ), as├Ł que no
    necesita el punto para justificar la sustituci├│n directa.
- retained learning:
  - cuando falta un dato (aqu├Ł el punto), elige el MENSAJE m├Īs fuerte que siga siendo sound SIN ese
    dato: "factorizar y cancelar" (v├Īlido siempre) en vez de "0/0 removible" (cierto solo en la
    ra├Łz). El alcance de la afirmaci├│n debe igualar lo verificable.
  - residual (pelda├▒o G2): mostrar la sustituci├│n CONCRETA (`x=2 ŌåÆ 5`) y la factorizaci├│n expl├Łcita
    (`(xŌłÆ1)(x+1)`) necesita el punto y el factor en el paso ŌĆö el cableado del punto sigue pendiente
    como el sub-ciclo arquitect├│nico que desbloquea L'H├┤pital/Taylor narrados y la justificaci├│n de
    impropias por dominancia.

    narran "Dominancia: grados iguales, cociente de coeficientes l├Łderes"; `(x+1)/(x┬▓+1) ŌåÆ 0`,

  - separar finito vs infinito en `notable_limit_name` evita falsos positivos cruzados: la misma
    forma racional significa cosas distintas en 0 (factor-cancela) y en Ōł× (dominancia).
- retained learning:

    equivocado para la misma forma estructural en reg├Łmenes distintos.
  - resumen del gatekeeper G2 tras 4 sub-ciclos: l├Łmites notables (├Ś6 equivalentes + (aßĄśŌłÆ1)/u +
    Euler + (1ŌłÆcos)/u┬▓), s├Īndwich, continuidad, factor-y-cancela y dominancia en infinito ya se
    narran, todo sound por chequeo de resultado/grado, huella NONE. Residual (pelda├▒o): mostrar la

    L'H├┤pital/Taylor narrados paso a paso, y dominancia exponencial (`e^(ŌłÆx)┬Ęp(x) ŌåÆ 0`).

## 2026-06-21 - Transcendental u-substitution by guess-and-verify (cos(x)┬Ęe^(sin x), etc.)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/symbolic_integration_support.rs`
    (`transcendental_chain_substitution_antiderivative`, registered late in the
    `integrate_symbolic_expr` dispatch; helpers `collect_chain_substitution_candidates`,
    `unary_chain_antiderivative`, `reduce_ln_e_and_unit_products`, `usub_strip_top_neg`)
- status:
  - `retained` (the "sustituci├│n-u general transcendente" P1 win; the highest-value residual
    integration family ŌĆö `g` polynomial already worked, `g` transcendental did not)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (new integration family)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 integration)
  - cell: `Ōł½cos(x)┬Ęe^(sin x) = e^(sin x)`, `Ōł½sin(x)┬Ęe^(cos x) = ŌłÆe^(cos x)`,
    `Ōł½e^x┬Ęcos(e^x) = sin(e^x)`, `Ōł½e^x┬Ęsin(e^x) = ŌłÆcos(e^x)`, `Ōł½sinh(x)┬Ęe^(cosh x) = e^(cosh x)`,
    `Ōł½cosh(x)┬Ęe^(sinh x) = e^(sinh x)`; scaled/affine-argument via the engine's linearity
    (`Ōł½2cos(2x)┬Ęe^(sin 2x) = e^(sin 2x)`).
  - behavior_change_expected: products `g'(x)┬Ęf(g(x))` with f Ōłł {exp,sin,cos,sinh,cosh} flip from
    residual to their elementary antiderivative. Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE
    (the matcher is late in the dispatch and accept-gated by an exact derivative check, so it only
    newly-supports residuals; no existing fixture moved). Workspace 12247 passed / 0 failed.
  - SOUNDNESS (gold standard): the matcher GUESSES `F(g)` and ACCEPTS only when
    `d/dx F(g) == integrand` EXACTLY ŌĆö the differentiation is the verifier. Adversarial round-trip
    holds for both signs and exp/trig/hyperbolic cofactors; non-elementary forms correctly decline
    (`exp(x┬▓)`, `exp(sin x)` (missing cofactor), `sin(x)/x`, `cos(x┬▓)` stay residual ŌĆö no
    fabrication).
- observed:
  - the exact-equality check needed two non-obvious folds: (1) differentiating `Pow(E, g)` via the
    GENERAL power rule leaves an unreduced `ln(E)` ( = 1 ) factor, and (2) the `Ōł½sin = ŌłÆcos` /
    odd-sign cases produce a `Neg` the integrand lacks. `reduce_ln_e_and_unit_products` folds
    `ln(E)ŌåÆ1` and unit products AND bubbles sign to the top; the compare then matches sign-stripped
    cores with `SemanticEqualityChecker::are_equal` and an explicit sign check.
  - `exprs_equivalent` (multipoly) and the block-12 verifier both DECLINE pure transcendental
    products (`e^(sin x)` is not a polynomial atom they reconcile); the structural+commutative
    `are_equal` on the normalized derivative is the check that works for them.
- retained learning:
  - guess-and-verify is the soundest integration strategy: produce a candidate, DIFFERENTIATE it,
    and accept only on exact match ŌĆö a wrong guess can never slip through because the verifier is
    the trusted differentiator. The hard part is the EXACT equality, not the guessing.
  - when comparing a differentiated candidate to an integrand, normalize the two differentiation
    ARTEFACTS first: `ln(E)=1` (from `Pow(E,┬Ę)` under the general power rule) and top-level sign.
    Both are exact, so the normalization is sound and unlocks `are_equal`.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): `g'` cofactors that differ from the literal `f(g)` derivative by a non-unit
    constant the engine doesn't pre-factor; `f(g)/x` log-substitution forms where the derivative's
    `1/x` is a `Mul`-reciprocal vs the integrand's `Div` (e.g. `sin(ln x)/x`); and outer `f` beyond
    exp/sin/cos/sinh/cosh (tan, ln, inverse-trig).

## 2026-06-21 - taylor()/series() around a non-zero expansion point
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/limits_support.rs` (`taylor_series_at_point_expr`)
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/rules/calculus/taylor.rs` (`TaylorRule` dispatches point 0 ŌåÆ
    analytic engine, point ŌēĀ 0 ŌåÆ definition; `try_extract_taylor_call` returns the point)
- status:
  - `retained` (extends the `taylor()`/`series()` command from Maclaurin-only to a general
    expansion point)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Taylor at a point)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `taylor(exp(x), x, 1, 3) = e┬Ę(1 + (xŌłÆ1) + (xŌłÆ1)┬▓/2 + (xŌłÆ1)┬│/6)`,
    `taylor(1/x, x, 1, 3) = 1 ŌłÆ (xŌłÆ1) + (xŌłÆ1)┬▓ ŌłÆ (xŌłÆ1)┬│`, `taylor(x^2, x, 1, 2) = x^2`. Point 0
    still routes to the analytic engine (nicer closed forms), unchanged.
  - behavior_change_expected: `taylor(f, x, a, n)` with a ŌēĀ 0 flips from residual to its Taylor
    polynomial in (xŌłÆa). Contract updated (the old "non-zero point declines" test became
    "accepts"). Guardrail huella NONE except `filtered_out +1` (the new cas_engine test); pressure
    NONE. Workspace 12248 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
  - SOUNDNESS: built straight from the definition `╬Ż f^(k)(a)/k!┬Ę(xŌłÆa)^k` using the trusted
    differentiator and exact substitution ŌĆö correct by construction (verified: `e^x` around 1 and
    `1/x` around 1 expand to the known series).
- observed:
  - the definition form (repeated differentiate + substitute x=a) sidesteps needing an
    analytic-shift engine; the unsimplified `f^(k)(a)` (e.g. `e^1`) is folded by the engine's
    simplifier downstream, so the rule can emit it raw.
  - skipping the final (unused) differentiation (`if k < order`) avoids aborting when `f^(order+1)`
    would be unavailable.
- retained learning:
  - when an analytic series engine is point-0-only, the general expansion point is cheap straight
    from the Taylor DEFINITION (derivatives at the point) ŌĆö no shift machinery, and sound by
    construction because the differentiator is trusted and substitution is exact.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): non-rational `a` gives transcendental coefficients (`sin(1)`); the output
    is correct but unfactored ŌĆö a cosmetic pelda├▒o.

## 2026-06-21 - Soundness fix: gcd/lcm of 3+ arguments ignored all but the first two
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/number_theory_support.rs` (`try_eval_simple_number_theory_call`: the
    `gcd`/`lcm` arms fold over ALL arguments)
  - `crates/cas_session_core/src/eval.rs` (arity gate `"gcd" | "lcm" => arity >= 2`)
- status:
  - `retained` (soundness / wrong-answer fix + capability: multi-arg lcm)
- capture:
  - investment_class: soundness (wrong-answer fix)
  - cell: `gcd(8, 12, 6) = 2` (was 4), `gcd(30, 20, 12) = 2` (was 10), `gcd(100, 60, 45) = 5`
    (was 20), `gcd(7, 11, 13) = 1`; `lcm(4, 6, 8) = 24` (was "lcm no definida"),
    `lcm(2, 3, 4, 5) = 60`. Two-argument `gcd(8,12)=4`, `lcm(4,6)=12` unchanged.
  - behavior_change_expected: `gcd`/`lcm` of 3+ integer arguments flips from a wrong value (or a
    not-defined error for lcm) to the correct fold. Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE.
    Workspace 12249 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the `gcd` arm matched `args.len() >= 2` but computed only `gcd(args[0], args[1])`, silently
    dropping the rest ŌĆö a wrong answer whenever the third+ argument tightened the gcd. `lcm` was
    gated to exactly 2 args, so 3+ errored. Both now fold `gcd(gcd(a,b),c,ŌĆ”)` /
    `lcm(lcm(a,b),c,ŌĆ”)` over the slice.
- retained learning:
  - an n-ary reduction guarded by `len() >= 2` but implemented on `args[0..2]` is a latent
    wrong-answer: the guard ADMITS the larger arities it then silently truncates. Match the
    implementation to the guard ŌĆö fold over the whole slice.
  - when fixing one n-ary op (gcd), check its siblings (lcm, and any min/max/and/or style reducer)
    for the same truncation; lcm had the dual bug (errored instead of folding).

    `sum(k┬Ę2^k, k, 2, 4) = 96` (symbolic lower bound), `sum(k┬Ę(1/2)^k, ŌĆ”)`. Bare geometric
    `sum(2^k) = 2^(n+1) ŌłÆ 2` and polynomial sums unchanged (the builder needs the `k┬Ę` cofactor).
  - behavior_change_expected: `sum(k┬Ęr^k)` with a symbolic bound flips from residual to its closed
    form via `T(m) = r(1 ŌłÆ (m+1)r^m + m┬Ęr^(m+1))/(1ŌłÆr)^2`, `╬Ż_{start}^{end} = T(end) ŌłÆ T(startŌłÆ1)`.

- observed:

    three-exhaustive-match churn for a narration label that is geometric-adjacent.
- retained learning:
  - a one-line-cofactor extension of a geometric family (`k┬Ęr^k` on top of `r^k`) costs only the
    closed form plus the existing geometric extractor; reuse the ratio extractor and the
    start-offset subtraction pattern already proven for the power sums.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): general affine cofactor `(╬▒k+╬▓)┬Ęr^k` (split as ╬▒┬Ę╬Żk┬Ęr^k + ╬▓┬Ę╬Żr^k) and
    higher-degree `k^2┬Ęr^k`.

## 2026-06-21 - Affine arithmetic-geometric: c┬Ęk┬Ęr^k and the distributed (╬▒k+╬▓)┬Ęr^k
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`try_build_arithmetic_geometric_sum` flattened via
    `mul_leaves` to absorb constant factors; new `try_build_geometric_additive_sum` for the
    distributed sum, dispatched after the arithmetic-geometric builder)
- status:
  - `retained` (completes the arithmetic-geometric family to the affine cofactor `(╬▒k+╬▓)┬Ęr^k`)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (closed-form coverage)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `sum(3┬Ęk┬Ę2^k, k, 1, n)` (folds to 102 at n=3), `sum((2k+1)┬Ę2^k, k, 1, n)` (= 82 at n=3),
    `sum((2k+1)┬Ę2^k, k, 2, 4) = 220` (symbolic lower bound). Bare geometric and polynomial sums
    unchanged.
  - behavior_change_expected: `c┬Ęk┬Ęr^k` and the affine `(╬▒k+╬▓)┬Ęr^k` (which the engine distributes
    to `r^k + ╬▒┬Ęk┬Ęr^(k+1)`) flip from residual to closed form. Guardrail + pressure huella
    structurally NONE. Workspace passed / 0 failed; verified by an independent brute force over the
    distributed forms ├Ś bounds.
- observed:
  - the engine DISTRIBUTES `(╬▒k+╬▓)┬Ęr^k` into a sum before the summation builders run, so the affine
    case is really a linearity over geometric + arithmetic-geometric TERMS ŌĆö handled by splitting
    the additive summand (`AddView`) and summing each term. The constant-coefficient `c┬Ęk┬Ęr^k`
    needed only flattening the product with `mul_leaves` and multiplying the coefficients.
  - the additive builder declines unless EVERY term is geometric / arithmetic-geometric, so
    polynomial sums (`k^2+k`) fall through untouched to their own linearity builder.
- retained learning:
  - when the engine pre-distributes a product (`(╬▒k+╬▓)┬Ęr^k ŌåÆ r^k + ╬▒┬Ęk┬Ęr^(k+1)`), the matcher must
    target the DISTRIBUTED form: split the additive summand and sum each term, rather than trying
    to match the factored product the user typed. A unit test that bypasses the engine's
    simplification (bare `parse`) sees the un-distributed form and misleads ŌĆö test the distributed
    shape the pipeline actually produces.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): higher-degree polynomial cofactors `k^2┬Ęr^k` (a second arithmetic-geometric
    order), and non-rational ratios.

## 2026-06-21 - G1 gatekeeper sub-ciclo: 1/(x^6-1) integra (subir el budget del zero-test)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/general_integration_backend/verification_algebraic.rs`
    (`algebraic_zero_test_budget`: max_terms 64ŌåÆ256, max_total_degree 16ŌåÆ32, max_pow_exp 16ŌåÆ24)
- status:
  - `retained` (primer sub-ciclo retenible del gatekeeper G1 "integraci├│n racional universal":
    los racionales de grado 6 que factorizan ENTEROS sobre ŌäÜ ya verifican)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (gatekeeper G1) ŌĆö la promesa "universal" en integraci├│n racional
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, gatekeeper G1)
  - cell: `integrate(1/(x^6-1)) = 1/6┬Ęln|xŌłÆ1| + 1/12┬Ęln(x┬▓ŌłÆx+1) ŌłÆ ŌĆ”` (round-trip `diff = 1/(x^6-1)`),
    `1/(x^6-64)`, `1/(2x^6-2)`. Honestidad preservada: `1/(x^8-1)`, `1/(x^6+1)`, `1/(x^5-1)`,
    `1/(x^4-4)` (cu├Īrticas IRREDUCIBLES sobre ŌäÜ, requieren factor-over-ŌäØ) siguen residuales.
  - behavior_change_expected: racionales de grado 6 cuyo denominador parte en lineales +
    cuadr├Īticas irreducibles sobre ŌäÜ pasan de residual a forma cerrada. Huella guardrail+pressure
    estructuralmente NONE (ninguna fixture cubr├Ła `1/(x^6-1)`). Workspace 12253 passed / 0 failed.
  - SOUNDNESS: el `algebraic_rational_zero_test` es una DECISI├ōN exacta (mapea ŌłÜc a un ├Ītomo t y
    reduce por t┬▓=c); subir el budget del multipoly NO puede crear falsos positivos ŌĆö solo permite
    decidir residuales m├Īs grandes. Round-trip diff verificado en los casos nuevos.
- observed:
  - DIAGN├ōSTICO CORREGIDO respecto al run anterior: el gap de `1/(x^6-1)` NO era un rationalizador
    de radicales faltante en el verifier (el `algebraic_rational_zero_test` YA hace ŌłÜcŌå”t, t┬▓=c). El
    verifier llegaba al zero-test (node_count 110 < cap 160, radicand ŌłÜ3 nonneg representado) pero
    `expr_to_rational` fallaba por el BUDGET del multipoly (max_terms 64 / max_total_degree 16): el
    residual de grado 6 sobre el denominador com├║n excede esos l├Łmites. Subirlos lo resuelve.
  - `1/(x^4+x^2+1)` (cu├Īrtica SG sola) ya verificaba porque su residual cab├Ła en el budget viejo;
    `1/(x^6-1)` a├▒ade dos factores lineales ŌåÆ residual mayor ŌåÆ no cab├Ła.
- retained learning:
  - antes de inventar un procedimiento de decisi├│n nuevo, COMPRUEBA si el existente ya cubre el
    caso y solo lo bloquea un budget/cap: instrumenta el punto de bail (node_count, radicands,
    expr_to_rational) en vez de asumir el peor caso. Aqu├Ł el "radical rationalizer M+" que parec├Ła
    necesario era en realidad un budget de 3 constantes.
  - subir un budget de un verificador EXACTO es soundness-safe (no a├▒ade falsos positivos, solo
    decide entradas mayores); el coste es perf, as├Ł que m├Łdelo con la huella de tiempo y mant├®n el
    cap del node_count como cota dura contra blow-ups.
  - residual (pelda├▒o G1): factor-over-ŌäØ para las cu├Īrticas irreducibles sobre ŌäÜ (`1/(x^8-1)`,
    `1/(x^6+1)`, `1/(x^5-1)` con ŌłÜ5, `1/(x^4-4)` con ŌłÜ2) ŌĆö el LRT / factorizaci├│n real es el
    siguiente sub-ciclo del gatekeeper, ahora s├Ł genuinamente net-new.

## 2026-06-21 - ╬Ż p(k)┬Ęr^k con cofactor polin├│mico de grado Ōēż2 (k┬▓┬Ęr^k y combinaciones)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs`
    (`try_build_arithmetic_geometric_sum` generalizado de cofactor lineal a polin├│mico Ōēż2;
    nuevos `geometric_sum_closed_form` (SŌéĆ), `arithmetic_geometric_square_closed_form` /
    `arithmetic_geometric_square_one_to` (SŌéé), helper `product_of_leaves`)
- status:
  - `retained` (capability P1 barata: `taylor()`/`series()` + LINEALIDAD de sumatorios ŌĆö extiende
    la suma aritm├®tico-geom├®trica de grado 1 a grado 2, net-new sobre maquinaria existente)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Fase 1, win P1 ŌĆö linealidad de sumatorios)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, ├Łtem "linealidad de sumatorios")
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (reusa `Polynomial::from_expr`, `extract_geometric_term`,
    `arithmetic_geometric_closed_form` ya existentes; el builder SŌéü viejo es ahora el caso ╬▓ del general)
  - cell: `sum(k┬▓┬Ę2^k, k, 1, n)`, `sum(k┬▓┬Ę3^k)`, `sum((2k┬▓ŌłÆ3k+1)┬Ę2^k)`, `sum((k┬▓+1)┬Ę2^k)`,
    `sum((k┬▓+2k)┬Ę2^k)` ŌåÆ forma cerrada exacta (verificado fold vs fuerza bruta en n concretos).
    El builder descompone p(k)=╬▒k┬▓+╬▓k+╬│ v├Ła `Polynomial::from_expr` y emite ╬▒┬ĘSŌéé + ╬▓┬ĘSŌéü + ╬│┬ĘSŌéĆ.
  - behavior_change_expected: los productos `cofactor_grado2 ┬Ę r^k` pasan de residual a forma cerrada.
    Cofactor grado 0 (geom├®trica pura) se declina ŌåÆ lo posee el builder geom├®trico; grado >2 o
    no-polin├│mico (1/k, ŌłÜk) ŌåÆ `from_expr` falla y declina. Huella guardrail+pressure estructuralmente
    NONE (ninguna fixture cubr├Ła k┬▓┬Ęr^k).
  - SOUNDNESS: las tres formas cerradas SŌéĆ, SŌéü, SŌéé son identidades exactas sobre `BigRational`;
    el test `quadratic_arithmetic_geometric_sums_match_brute_force` las pliega a n concreto y compara
    con la suma directa. El gate de fase NO aplica (regla sint├Īctica/de presentaci├│n, sin dominio de
    valores: no enhebra `ValueDomain` ŌĆö ser├Ła ceremonia muerta).
- observed:
  - el motor PRE-FACTORIZA el sumando: `(k┬▓+1)┬Ę2^k` llega como `2^k┬Ę(k┬▓+1)`, y la suma
    `k┬▓┬Ę2^k + k┬Ę2^k` se factoriza a `2^k┬Ę(k┬▓+k)`. `mul_leaves` separa el factor geom├®trico del
    cofactor, que se reconstruye con `product_of_leaves` y se pasa a `Polynomial::from_expr` (que
    EXPANDE Mul/Pow, p.ej. `k┬Ę(k+1)ŌåÆk┬▓+k`). El nuevo builder cubre ambas representaciones.
  - RESIDUAL HONESTO (pelda├▒o, NO regresi├│n): los cofactores PR├ōNICOS `k(k┬▒1)=k┬▓┬▒k` quedan
    residuales END TO END (`sum((k┬▓+k)┬Ę2^k)` ŌåÆ `sum(2^k┬Ę(k┬▓+k))`) aunque el builder los resuelve
    bien. Causa: el cofactor lleva factor com├║n `k`, as├Ł que el sumando OSCILA entre la forma
    factorizada `2^k┬Ęk┬Ę(k+1)` y la distribuida `k┬▓┬Ę2^k+k┬Ę2^k` durante la normalizaci├│n del sub├Īrbol
    del sumando, y el nodo `sum` nunca se estabiliza ŌåÆ el orquestador descarta la forma cerrada
    (que es CORRECTA: `SumRule` la produce, fold=124 en n=3). `k┬▓+2k=k(k+2)` S├Ź funciona (no es
    pr├│nico), as├Ł que el gap es estrecho. Es un artefacto del orquestador (factorŌåödistribuye del
    sumando), preexistente y net-cero (esos casos ya eran residuales antes del ciclo).
- retained learning:
  - cuando un builder de familia ya existe para grado d, generalizar a grado d+1 suele ser
    "extraer el cofactor como `Polynomial` y combinar linealmente las sumas base SßĄó" ŌĆö m├Īs barato y
    m├Īs general que un matcher nuevo por forma. El builder lineal viejo es el caso ╬▓ del general.
  - una capacidad nueva puede EXPONER una oscilaci├│n factorŌåödistribuye preexistente del orquestador
    sin introducirla: si el output no cambia (residual antes y despu├®s) NO es regresi├│n. P├Łnchalo con
    un test que demuestre que el BUILDER es exacto (fold vs fuerza bruta) para que un fix futuro del
    orquestador aterrice un resultado verificado, no un bug nuevo. La frontera del gap (pr├│nicos
    k(k┬▒1), cofactor con factor com├║n de ├Łndice) es la intenci├│n declarada del siguiente pelda├▒o.
  - RESIDUAL HONESTO #2 (preexistente, NO de este ciclo): la ratio FRACCIONARIA en grado 2 queda
    residual end-to-end (`sum(k┬▓┬Ę(1/2)^k)` ŌåÆ `sum(1/2^k┬Ęk┬▓)`). El builder es exacto (el test de
    grado 1 cubre ratio 1/2), pero el motor normaliza la entrada de grado 2 a la forma Div/potencia-
    negativa `k┬▓/2^k`, y `k/2^k` (grado 1, forma Div) YA era residual antes del ciclo ŌåÆ es un gap de
    NORMALIZACI├ōN de representaci├│n (Div vs `(1/2)^k` expl├Łcito), no de la suma. `k┬Ę(1/2)^k` (forma
    expl├Łcita) s├Ł cierra. Net-cero respecto al ciclo.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): (1) estabilizar la normalizaci├│n factor/distribuye del sumando para los
    pr├│nicos k(k┬▒1) ŌĆö concern del orquestador, clase A, no de la familia de sumas; (2) normalizar la
    forma Div/potencia-negativa del factor geom├®trico (`k^p/2^k`) a `(1/2)^k` antes de `SumRule` para
    cerrar la ratio fraccionaria en grado Ōēź1-Div y grado 2; (3) cofactor polin├│mico de grado Ōēź3 (SŌéÜ
    general v├Ła el operador (r┬Ęd/dr)^p sobre ╬Żr^k) si la cola lo pide.

- area:

    (`try_build_arithmetic_geometric_sum`: el bucle de hojas acepta una hoja `Div(num, den)` con

    `p(k)/r^k`, p.ej. el cl├Īsico `╬Ż k/2^k` finito ŌĆö antes residual)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Fase 1, win P1 ŌĆö linealidad/cierre de sumatorios)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (continuaci├│n directa del ciclo de cofactor grado-2)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (reusa `extract_geometric_term` sobre el DENOMINADOR; el resto
    del builder grado-Ōēż2 ya estaba)
  - cell: `sum(k/2^k, k, 1, n)` (`╬Ż k/2^k 1..4 = 13/8` verificado), `sum(k/3^k)`, `sum((2k+1)/2^k)`,
    `sum(3k/2^k)`, `sum((k+1)/2^k)`, cota inferior 0/1 ŌåÆ forma cerrada exacta (fold vs fuerza bruta
    BigRational). `extract_geometric_term` lee la ratio 1/r del denominador y el numerador es el cofactor.
  - behavior_change_expected: los cocientes `p(k)/r^k` (p polinomio grado 1, r entero Ōēź2) pasan de
    residual a forma cerrada. Huella guardrail+pressure estructuralmente NONE.
  - SOUNDNESS: misma identidad exacta `BigRational` que el ciclo previo; el cociente solo cambia la
    ratio a 1/r v├Ła `recip()`. Regla sint├Īctica/de presentaci├│n ŌåÆ el gate de fase NO aplica.
- observed:
  - `mul_leaves` NO descompone `Div`: `k/2^k` llega como una sola hoja `Div(k, 2^k)`, por eso el
    guard `len < 2` la rechazaba antes de mirarla. `extract_geometric_term` ya cubr├Ła `Div(CONST,
    geom)` (`1/2^k`); el hueco era `Div(POLINOMIO, geom)`.
  - RESIDUAL HONESTO (pelda├▒o, NO regresi├│n): la ratio fraccionaria en GRADO 2 (`k┬▓/2^k`,
    `k┬▓┬Ę(1/2)^k`) sigue residual. El builder produce el candidato correcto (ratio 1/2, SŌéé), pero el
    orquestador OSCILA entre las formas Div / potencia-negativa / `(1/2)^k` del factor geom├®trico y
    el nodo `sum` no se estabiliza ŌĆö MISMA clase que la oscilaci├│n pr├│nica del ciclo anterior
    (concern del orquestador, clase A, no de la familia de sumas). Grado 1 fraccionario s├Ł converge.
  - RESIDUAL HONESTO #2 (pelda├▒o): la suma INFINITA aritm├®tico-geom├®trica convergente (`╬Ż_{1}^{Ōł×}
    k/2^k = 2`) sigue residual: `try_convergent_infinite_geometric_sum` solo cubre la geom├®trica
    pura; extenderla al caso aritm├®tico-geom├®trico es un pelda├▒o separado y acotado.
- retained learning:
  - una FAMILIA de suma escrita como cociente vs producto son la misma matem├Ītica pero distinto
    ├Īrbol: `mul_leaves` parte `Mul` pero no `Div`, as├Ł que un guard `len < 2` esconde el caso
    cociente de una sola hoja. Al a├▒adir una familia producto, comprueba siempre la graf├Ła `Div`
    equivalente (a menudo es la forma can├│nica del motor para ratio < 1).
  - el l├Łmite del cierre end-to-end no es el builder sino la convergencia del ORQUESTADOR: grado 1
    fraccionario converge, grado 2 fraccionario no (misma oscilaci├│n que los pr├│nicos). La frontera
    "grado del cofactor ├Ś representaci├│n del factor geom├®trico" es la intenci├│n del pelda├▒o de
    estabilizaci├│n del orquestador (clase A) que unificar├Ła pr├│nicos + fraccionario-grado-2.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): (1) suma infinita aritm├®tico-geom├®trica convergente (`╬Ż k┬Ęr^k`, |r|<1);
    (2) la estabilizaci├│n factorŌåödistribuye / DivŌåöpotencia-negativa del orquestador que cerrar├Ła
    a la vez pr├│nicos k(k┬▒1) y fraccionario grado 2.

  - secondary_dimension: didactic_value (cubre la familia escalada con una sola generalizaci├│n)
  - cell: `limit(sin(3x)/x, x, 0) = 3` narra "l├Łm(uŌåÆ0) sin(3┬Ęu)/u = 3"; `tan(2x)/x ŌåÆ 2`,
    `arctan(5x)/x ŌåÆ 5`, escala fraccionaria `sin(x/2)/x ŌåÆ 1/2`, escala negativa `sin(-2x)/x ŌåÆ -2`.
    El notable base `sin(u)/u = 1` es el caso a=1. Cubre la familia de equivalentes de primer orden
    (sin, tan, arcsin, arctan, sinh, tanh, asinh, atanh).

    que fijaba `sin(2x)/x ŌåÆ 2` como NO narrado ahora lo narra (era una decisi├│n conservadora, no
    soundness ŌĆö `sin(au)/u = a┬Ęsin(au)/(au) ŌåÆ a` es un notable est├Īndar).
  - SOUNDNESS: la narraci├│n solo dispara cuando el RESULTADO es exactamente la escala a (`after_is(
    scale)`), as├Ł que `sin(2x)/x ŌåÆ 3` (fabricado, el real es 2) y `sin(x)/x` en x=5 (ŌåÆ sin(5)/5)
    declinan. `limit_linear_scale` exige forma `a┬Ęu` pura (sin t├®rmino constante): `sin(x+1)/x`
    declina. Sin dominio de valores ŌåÆ el gate de fase NO aplica (narraci├│n).
- observed:
  - el notable base ya exist├Ła pero EXIG├ŹA `arg == u` (compare_expr Equal); el hueco era el factor de
    escala. `Polynomial::from_expr(arg, u)` con grado 1 y t├®rmino constante 0 da la escala exacta y
    cubre `3x`, `x*3`, `-x`, `x/2` de un golpe ŌĆö m├Īs robusto que un matcher Mul a mano.
  - el orden importa: la rama escalada (escala = a) va ANTES que las ramas `after==1` (ln(1+u)/u,
    (e^uŌłÆ1)/u) porque esas no son `first_order_equivalent_name` (Ln/exp) ni forma `a┬Ęu`, as├Ł que no
    colisionan; ln(1+u) tiene t├®rmino constante ŌåÆ `limit_linear_scale` la declina.
- retained learning:
  - una narraci├│n "solo la forma base" (arg == u) suele generalizar a "la forma con par├Īmetro"
    (arg == a┬Ęu) leyendo el par├Īmetro con `Polynomial::from_expr` y exigiendo que el resultado lo
    confirme exactamente ŌĆö la confirmaci├│n por resultado ES el gate de soundness de la narraci├│n
    (no fabrica un valor, lo verifica contra el l├Łmite ya calculado por el motor).

    ŌåÆ 1/2` (equivalente de primer orden de la ra├Łz / racionalizaci├│n); (3) `(1+1/x)^x ŌåÆ e` y
    `ln(x)/x ŌåÆ 0` (xŌåÆŌł×) sin substep ŌĆö notables/dominancias a├║n no narrados.

## 2026-06-21 - G2 narrativa: l├Łmite notable cruzado f(a┬Ęu)/g(b┬Ęu) ŌåÆ a/b
- area:
  - `crates/cas_didactic/src/didactic/focused_rule_substeps.rs`
    (`notable_limit_name`: rama nueva para `num/den` con den NO bare-var; helpers
    `linear_scale_of`, `limit_single_variable_name`, `limit_first_order_factor`; `limit_linear_scale`

- status:

- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (gatekeeper G2 ŌĆö narraci├│n educativa de l├Łmites)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, G2)
  - secondary_dimension: didactic_value (cubre dos familias ŌĆödenominador escalado y cociente de dos
    notablesŌĆö con una sola generalizaci├│n por escala lineal)
  - cell: `sin(3x)/(2x) ŌåÆ 3/2` narra "l├Łm(uŌåÆ0) sin(3┬Ęu)/(2┬Ęu) = 3/2"; `sin(x)/(2x) ŌåÆ 1/2`;
    `tan(3x)/sin(2x) ŌåÆ 3/2` (cociente de DOS notables); `arcsin(x)/(5x) ŌåÆ 1/5`. Cada lado aporta su
    escala lineal: bare `b┬Ęu` ŌåÆ b, notable `g(b┬Ęu)` ŌåÆ b (porque `g(bu) ~ bu`).
  - behavior_change_expected: los cocientes notables cruzados pasan de sin-substep a narrados.
    Huella guardrail+pressure NONE.
  - SOUNDNESS: narra solo cuando el resultado es exactamente a/b (`after_is(ratio)`), as├Ł que
    `sin(3x)/(2x) ŌåÆ 5` (fabricado) declina y en punto ŌēĀ 0 el resultado no es a/b limpio ŌåÆ declina.
    Exige Ōēź1 lado notable genuino (`num_fn.is_some() || den_fn.is_some()`): `cos(2x)/(3x)` (cos sin
    equivalente de primer orden) y `2x/(3x)` (cancelaci├│n trivial) declinan. Sin dominio ŌåÆ sin gate.
- observed:
  - el notable base usaba `den == Variable`; el cruzado necesita la VARIABLE del l├Łmite, que
    `notable_limit_name` no recibe. `cas_ast::traversal::collect_variables` da el ├║nico nombre de
    variable (univariable) sin enhebrar la firma. `Polynomial::from_expr(lado, u)` con grado 1 y
    t├®rmino constante 0 da la escala de cada lado uniformemente (bare o argumento de notable).
  - el orden de ramas importa: la cruzada va tras `den==Variable` y `(1ŌłÆcos)/u┬▓`, antes de
    factor-y-cancela; los casos polin├│micos (`(x┬▓ŌłÆ1)/(xŌłÆ1)`) tienen `linear_scale_of = None` (gradoŌēĀ1
    o t├®rmino constante) as├Ł que no colisionan y caen a factor-cancela.
- retained learning:
  - cuando una narraci├│n de cociente se generaliza de "den = u" a "den = b┬Ęu" (o a "ambos lados son
    notables"), modela cada LADO como (escala, nombre-opcional) y narra la raz├│n de escalas: una
    abstracci├│n ("factor de primer orden") cubre denominador-escalado y cociente-de-dos-notables a la
    vez. La funci├│n notable y la bare `b┬Ęu` son el mismo objeto (escala) a efectos del l├Łmite.
  - obtener la variable del l├Łmite v├Ła `collect_variables` (univariable ŌćÆ exactamente una) evita
    propagar la variable por toda la firma de los narradores ŌĆö barato y local.
  - residual (pelda├▒o G2): `(ŌłÜ(1+u)ŌłÆ1)/u ŌåÆ 1/2` (equivalente de la ra├Łz / racionalizaci├│n);
    `(1+1/x)^x ŌåÆ e` y `ln(x)/x ŌåÆ 0` en Ōł× (notable/dominancia logar├Łtmica a├║n sin narrar);
    L'H├┤pital/Taylor paso a paso y el cableado del PUNTO del l├Łmite (arquitect├│nico).

  - secondary_dimension: didactic_value (una clasificaci├│n de crecimiento cubre 6+ formas cruzadas)
  - cell: `ln(x)/x ŌåÆ 0`, `ln(x)┬▓/x ŌåÆ 0`, `ŌłÜx/ln(x) ŌåÆ Ōł×`, `x┬▓/e╦Ż ŌåÆ 0`, `e╦Ż/x┬│ ŌåÆ Ōł×`, `ln(x)/e╦Ż ŌåÆ 0`
    narran la jerarqu├Ła ("X crece m├Īs despacio/r├Īpido que Y (ln Ōē¬ potencia Ōē¬ exp)"). La dominancia
    polin├│mica (grados) ya exist├Ła; esto a├▒ade las clases log y exp.

    para la mayor sobre la menor); la clase exp exige `e^{p(var)}` con gradoŌēź1 y coeficiente l├Łder
    POSITIVO (as├Ł `e^{ŌłÆx}` no se clasifica como exp y `x┬▓/e^{ŌłÆx}` declina); `sin(x)/x` (sin no est├Ī
    en la jerarqu├Ła) declina. Narraci├│n pura ŌåÆ el gate de fase NO aplica.
- observed:
  - el `Polynomial::from_expr(num).ok()?` previo retornaba None en cuanto un lado no era polinomio
    (`ln(x)`, `e╦Ż`), as├Ł que la rama cruzada DEBE ir antes; las mismas-clase (potencia/potencia)
    caen a la comparaci├│n de grados existente intacta.
  - `sqrt(x)` en el `before` del l├Łmite queda como `Function(sqrt)` (el simplificador no siempre lo
    reescribe a `x^(1/2)`), mientras que `x^(1/2)` expl├Łcito s├Ł es `Pow`: hubo que a├▒adir el arm
    `BuiltinFn::Sqrt`. Lecci├│n: clasificar por forma debe cubrir AMBAS graf├Łas (funci├│n y potencia).
- retained learning:
  - una "jerarqu├Ła de dominancia" se modela como un `enum` ordenado (Ord derivado) + un clasificador
    por expresi├│n; la narraci├│n es entonces `clase(num) vs clase(den)` confirmada por el resultado.
    M├Īs general y robusto que un matcher por cada par de formas.
  - el resultado como or├Īculo de soundness escala tambi├®n a las dominancias: no se decide el l├Łmite,
    se nombra la jerarqu├Ła que el motor ya resolvi├│ (0 / ┬▒Ōł×).
  - residual (pelda├▒o G2): forma PRODUCTO `e^{ŌłÆx}┬Ęx┬▓ ŌåÆ 0`, `x┬Ęe^{ŌłÆx} ŌåÆ 0` (no es cociente; el

    `b^x` (b>1) adem├Īs de `e╦Ż`; L'H├┤pital paso a paso.

- status:

  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, G2)
  - secondary_dimension: didactic_value (un check cubre ra├Łz cuadrada, c├║bica y potencias enteras)
  - cell: `(ŌłÜ(1+x)ŌłÆ1)/x ŌåÆ 1/2` narra "l├Łm(uŌåÆ0) ((1+u)^(1/2) ŌłÆ 1)/u = 1/2 (equivalente (1+u)^a ~
    1 + a┬Ęu)"; `((1+x)^3ŌłÆ1)/x ŌåÆ 3`; `((1+x)^(1/2)ŌłÆ1)/x ŌåÆ 1/2`. Generaliza a CUALQUIER exponente
    racional a: la graf├Ła ŌłÜ (Function sqrt) y la Pow `(1+u)^a` ambas cubiertas.
  - behavior_change_expected: el equivalente binomial pasa de sin-substep a narrado. Huella NONE.
  - SOUNDNESS: narra solo si el resultado == a (el exponente); estructura exige base EXACTA `1+u`
    (`ŌłÜ(2+x)` declina) y t├®rmino restado 1; `a=0` se salta (trivial). Narraci├│n pura ŌåÆ sin gate.
- observed:
  - el motor NO computa `((1+x)^(1/3)ŌłÆ1)/x` (ra├Łz c├║bica) ŌĆö queda residual, as├Ł que no hay step que
    narrar (limitaci├│n del EVALUADOR de l├Łmites, no del narrador). El narrador solo nombra lo que el
    motor ya resolvi├│: ŌłÜ y potencias enteras s├Ł, ra├Łz c├║bica no (pelda├▒o del evaluador).
  - la graf├Ła importa otra vez: `ŌłÜ(1+u)` es `Function(sqrt)` y `(1+u)^(1/2)` es `Pow` ŌĆö el helper
    cubre ambas (mismo aprendizaje que el sqrt de la dominancia del ciclo previo).
- retained learning:
  - los equivalentes de primer orden `f(u) ~ a┬Ęu` se narran con un solo patr├│n "extrae a, conf├Łrmalo
    contra el resultado": `sin(au)/uŌåÆa`, `(aßĄśŌłÆ1)/uŌåÆln(a)`, `((1+u)^aŌłÆ1)/uŌåÆa` son la misma forma
    "lee el par├Īmetro, el resultado es el or├Īculo". La librer├Ła de notables converge a esa plantilla.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): el EVALUADOR de l├Łmites no computa ra├Łces no-cuadradas `(1+x)^(1/n)` ni la
    forma producto `e^{ŌłÆx}┬Ęx┬▓`; narrar L'H├┤pital paso a paso; cablear el PUNTO del l├Łmite.

## 2026-06-21 - Suma INFINITA aritm├®tico-geom├®trica convergente ╬Ż_{k=a}^Ōł× p(k)┬Ęr^k (|r|<1)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs`
    (`try_convergent_infinite_arithmetic_geometric_sum` + wiring en la rama de cota infinita;
    REFACTOR: extra├Łdo `decompose_arithmetic_geometric` compartido por el builder finito y el infinito)
- status:
  - `retained` (capability: cierra la serie infinita aritm├®tico-geom├®trica convergente, antes residual)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Fase 1, win P1 ŌĆö sumatorios; pelda├▒o declarado del run previo)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, sumatorios)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (las colas infinitas son las identidades finitas con r^nŌåÆ0;
    el REFACTOR DRY hace que finito e infinito compartan la descomposici├│n)
  - cell: `╬Ż k/2^k = 2`, `╬Ż k┬▓/2^k = 6`, `╬Ż k┬Ę(1/2)^k = 2`, `╬Ż_{0} k/3^k = 3/4`, `╬Ż (2k+1)/2^k = 5`,
    `╬Ż_{2} k/3^k = 5/12` ŌĆö resultado RACIONAL exacto. Colas kŌēź1: `r/(1ŌłÆr)`, `r/(1ŌłÆr)┬▓`, `r(1+r)/(1ŌłÆr)┬│`;
    correcci├│n de cota inferior por el head finito `╬Ż_{1}^{a-1}` (o el t├®rmino k=0 = ╬│ si a=0).
  - behavior_change_expected: las series infinitas `p(k)┬Ęr^k` con |r|<1 pasan de residual a un n├║mero.
    Huella guardrail+pressure NONE (ninguna fixture cubr├Ła infinito arith-geo).
  - SOUNDNESS: todo `BigRational` exacto; convergencia gateada por `|r|<1` (divergente declina ŌåÆ cae a
    `classify_infinite_sum` ŌåÆ Ōł×); cota inferior no-entera o negativa ŌåÆ residual honesto. Test compara
    las formas cerradas con sus valores conocidos y verifica el rechazo divergente (`╬Ż k┬Ę2^k`).
- observed:
  - la serie INFINITA convergente es m├Īs simple que la finita: el resultado es un N├ÜMERO (no expr
    simb├│lica), porque los t├®rminos r^n, r^(n+1) de la f├│rmula finita se anulan con |r|<1. Las colas
    quedan como tres rationals; la cota inferior se corrige con un head finito por fuerza bruta (a-1
    t├®rminos) o el t├®rmino k=0.
  - el REFACTOR `decompose_arithmetic_geometric` (descomposici├│n c┬Ęp(k)┬Ęr^k ŌåÆ (c, r, [╬│,╬▓,╬▒]),
    incluida la graf├Ła Div `k/2^k`) se reusa entre el builder finito y el infinito sin duplicar.
- retained learning:
  - cuando ya existe el builder FINITO de una familia, el INFINITO convergente suele ser "toma el
    l├Łmite nŌåÆŌł× de la forma cerrada": con |r|<1 los t├®rminos de borde se anulan y queda un valor
    exacto. Reusar la descomposici├│n (no la construcci├│n simb├│lica) es el corte natural.
  - antes de a├▒adir el caso infinito, EXTRAE la descomposici├│n compartida ŌĆö evita el duplicado que
    el ciclo previo tuvo que limpiar a posteriori (lecci├│n de dedup aplicada por adelantado).
  - residual (pelda├▒o): cofactor de grado Ōēź3 (colas SŌéÜ generales); cota inferior simb├│lica;
    `╬Ż p(k)┬Ęr^k` con r irracional; y la forma producto `e^{ŌłÆx}┬Ęx┬▓` para el narrador de l├Łmites.

## 2026-06-21 - P0 SOUNDNESS: suma telesc├│pica con gap Ōēź 2 daba RESPUESTA INCORRECTA
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`try_build_telescoping_rational_sum`, ruta general
    de factores lineales `(k+b)(k+c)`)
- status:
  - `retained` (FIX de soundness P0 ŌĆö wrong-answer; precede a toda capacidad)
- capture:
  - investment_class: soundness (wrong-answer en `sum`)
  - primary_dimension: correctness (cualquier comando, exento de la restricci├│n de fase)
  - cell: `sum(1/(k(k+2)), k, 1, inf)` daba **1/2**, correcto **3/4**; `[1,4]` daba **5/12**, correcto
    **17/30**; `sum(1/(k(k+3)))` daba **1/3**, correcto **11/18**; `1/(k(k+4))` daba 1/4, correcto 25/48.
  - behavior_change_expected: las telesc├│picas racionales `1/((k+b)(k+c))` con gap |cŌłÆb| Ōēź 2 pasan de
    un valor INCORRECTO al correcto, finito e infinito. Huella guardrail+pressure NONE (ninguna fixture
    de las lanes cubr├Ła gap Ōēź 2 ŌĆö el bug estaba en comportamiento de usuario no capturado).
  - SOUNDNESS: la identidad `1/((k+b)(k+c)) = 1/(cŌłÆb)┬Ę(1/(k+b) ŌłÆ 1/(k+c))` con gap d = cŌłÆb telescopia
    dejando d t├®rminos de borde en CADA extremo: `(1/d)┬Ę[╬Ż_{j=0}^{d-1} 1/(start+b+j) ŌłÆ ╬Ż_{j=0}^{d-1}
    1/(end+b+1+j)]`. La f├│rmula vieja usaba UN solo t├®rmino por extremo (correcto solo para d=1).
    Barrido de fuerza bruta: 108 casos (gaps 1-5, varios start/n, finito+infinito), 0 fallos.
- observed:
  - el bug solo afectaba la ruta GENERAL (gap arbitrario); las rutas `detect_consecutive` (gap 1) y
    `detect_affine_consecutive_..._and_gap` (consecutivos afines, p.ej. `(2k+1)(2k+3)`) eran correctas
    (un t├®rmino por extremo ES correcto para gap 1). El infinito reusaba la f├│rmula finita v├Ła
    `lim_{nŌåÆŌł×}`, as├Ł que el valor incorrecto se propagaba a ambos.
  - los tests existentes NO encodaban el valor incorrecto (solo cubr├Łan gap 1), as├Ł que el fix no rompi├│
    ning├║n contrato ŌĆö confirma que era un hueco de cobertura, no una decisi├│n.
- retained learning:
  - una f├│rmula de telescopio "primer t├®rmino ŌłÆ ├║ltimo t├®rmino" es un ERROR para gap Ōēź 2: el n├║mero de
    t├®rminos de borde que NO se cancelan es exactamente el gap. Cualquier sumatorio/telescopio nuevo
    debe testearse con FUERZA BRUTA a gap variable, no solo gap 1 ŌĆö el caso gap-1 esconde el bug.
  - un wrong-answer puede sobrevivir a una huella verde si ninguna fixture cubre el r├®gimen (aqu├Ł
    gap Ōēź 2): los fixtures de soundness deben barrer el PAR├üMETRO (gap), no un ├║nico representante.
  - residual (pelda├▒o): telesc├│picas con denominador EXPANDIDO `1/(k┬▓ŌłÆ1)`, `1/(4k┬▓ŌłÆ1)` (el motor
    expande `(k-1)(k+1)ŌåÆk┬▓ŌłÆ1` antes del builder; factorizar el cuadr├Ītico es el siguiente sub-ciclo).

  - cell: `╬Ż_{2}^Ōł× 1/(k┬▓ŌłÆ1) = 3/4`, `╬Ż_{3}^Ōł× 1/(k┬▓ŌłÆ4) = 25/48`, `1/(k┬▓+3k+2) ŌåÆ 1/2`,
    `╬Ż_{4}^Ōł× 1/(k┬▓ŌłÆ5k+6) = 1`. El motor EXPANDE `(k-1)(k+1)ŌåÆk┬▓ŌłÆ1`; el builder requer├Ła el producto.

    distintas pasan de residual a forma cerrada (finito e infinito). Huella NONE.

    (semilla f64 + correcci├│n por bucle; la decisi├│n keep/drop es `root*root == disc`, nunca f64).
    `4k┬▓ŌłÆ1` (no m├│nica), `k┬▓+1`/`k┬▓+k+1` (irreducibles) declinan ŌåÆ residuales honestos. Barrido de
    fuerza bruta finito+infinito 0 fallos.
- observed:

  - este ciclo DEPEND├ŹA del fix P0 anterior: factorizar `k┬▓ŌłÆ1`ŌåÆ`(k-1)(k+1)` (gap 2) sin el
    multi-t├®rmino de borde habr├Ła dado un valor incorrecto. Capacidad sobre soundness, en orden.
- retained learning:

    graf├Ła Div de las geom├®tricas, o sqrt funci├│n vs potencia). Factorizar el cuadr├Ītico es barato.
  - una ra├Łz cuadrada entera para una decisi├│n exacta NO usa f64 como gate: f64 es solo semilla,
    el gate es `root*root == disc` en enteros (regla de soundness: nunca f64 para keep/drop).

    af├Łn existente); ra├Łces racionales no enteras; denominador c├║bico+ con factores telesc├│picos.

## 2026-06-21 - G2 narrativa: dominancia en forma PRODUCTO p(x)┬Ęe^{-q(x)} ŌåÆ 0
- area:
  - `crates/cas_didactic/src/didactic/focused_rule_substeps.rs`
    (`limit_infinity_dominance`: rama de producto; helper `limit_is_decaying_exponential`)
- status:
  - `retained` (sub-ciclo G2: narra la dominancia exponencial en forma producto, antes sin substep)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (gatekeeper G2 ŌĆö narraci├│n educativa de l├Łmites)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, G2)
  - secondary_dimension: didactic_value (reusa `limit_growth_class` del ciclo 1 para clasificar los
    factores no-exponenciales)
  - cell: `x┬▓┬Ęe^{-x} ŌåÆ 0`, `x┬Ęe^{-x} ŌåÆ 0`, `xŌüĄ┬Ęe^{-2x} ŌåÆ 0`, `e^{-x┬▓}┬Ęx┬│ ŌåÆ 0` narran "la exponencial
    decae m├Īs r├Īpido de lo que crece la potencia ŌåÆ producto ŌåÆ 0". Antes el narrador solo ve├Ła
    cocientes (`x┬▓/e^x`); esta es la graf├Ła PRODUCTO.
  - behavior_change_expected: los productos polinomio├Śexponencial-decreciente en Ōł× pasan de
    sin-substep a narrados. Huella NONE.
  - SOUNDNESS: narra solo si el resultado es 0 Y hay un factor exponencial DECRECIENTE genuino
    (`e^{q}` con qŌåÆŌłÆŌł×: gradoŌēź1, coeficiente l├Łder NEGATIVO) Y todo otro factor es sub-exponencial
    (Power/Log/constante). `x┬▓┬Ęe^x ŌåÆ Ōł×` (exp creciente) y `sin(x)┬Ęe^{-x}` (sin no clasificado)
    declinan. Requiere Ōēź2 factores. Narraci├│n pura ŌåÆ sin gate de fase.
- observed:
  - el narrador de dominancia solo cubr├Ła `num/den`; `x┬▓┬Ęe^{-x}` es un Mul (no Div) y ca├Ła a None.
    La forma producto es el complemento natural del cociente: clasificar cada factor y exigir
    exactamente un decreciente-exponencial con el resto sub-exponencial.
  - reusa `limit_growth_class` (ciclo 1) para los factores no-exp; solo a├▒ade el reconocedor de
    exponencial DECRECIENTE (coeficiente l├Łder negativo, sim├®trico al creciente del ciclo 1).
- retained learning:
  - cuando un narrador de cociente est├Ī hecho, la graf├Ła PRODUCTO equivalente (`p┬Ęe^{-x}` = `p/e^x`)
    suele ser un sub-ciclo barato: descomponer en factores y clasificar, con el resultado como
    or├Īculo. El par creciente/decreciente de la exponencial es sim├®trico (signo del coef. l├Łder).
  - residual (pelda├▒o G2): producto en 0+ `x┬Ęln(x) ŌåÆ 0` (mecanismo distinto, no es Ōł×); bases `b^x`
    (b<1) decrecientes; L'H├┤pital/Taylor paso a paso para `(e╦ŻŌłÆ1ŌłÆx)/x┬▓ ŌåÆ 1/2`.

## 2026-06-21 - Telesc├│pica NO m├│nica v├Ła factores afines 1/(4k┬▓ŌłÆ1) = (2kŌłÆ1)(2k+1)
- area:

- status:
